title: Heat wave in Japan causes temperatures of 40°C, deaths and hospitalizations
summary: The country records hundreds of heatstroke cases and at least four deaths, while authorities forecast that high temperatures will persist in August.

title: China conducts live-fire exercises in Japan's exclusive economic zone and Tokyo files a protest
summary: Japan denounces the action as dangerous for navigation, while China defends the legality of its military maneuvers.

title: Japan's prime minister sparks controversy by claiming he sleeps between 0 and 3 hours a day
summary: The leader's posts about his lack of sleep spark debate about his health and management ability.

title: Ansei and Kotoei lead the Nagoya sumo tournament while Takayasu achieves his first winning record in three tournaments
summary: On the tenth day, veteran wrestler Takayasu secures his positive balance and Ansei maintains the lead after defeating Toyonoshoryu.

title: Average assets of Japanese lawmakers increase to 32.8 million yen
summary: The asset declaration shows that the richest lawmaker is Aizawa of the LDP, with real estate properties in Tokyo.

title: Takashi's government presents its first economic policy framework with a shift toward growth
summary: The new 'Basic Framework' abandons the priority of fiscal consolidation to boost the economy under the new government.

title: Ukraine attacks Russian oil facilities with drones in response to the offensive
summary: Massive bombings cause fuel supply disruptions and increase pressure on the Kremlin.

title: Asamonomori school punishes soccer students for drinking and smoking but does not withdraw from national tournament
summary: The school decides not to withdraw from the Inter-High despite the suspensions for inappropriate conduct.

title: NHK producer Yukiko Kobayashi, responsible for successful series like 'Oshin', dies
summary: The veteran producer, aged 86, left an indelible mark on Japanese public television.

title: Business association Kankeiren dismisses its secretary general for sexual harassment
summary: The decision is based on an external investigation that confirmed the employees' complaints.

title: The Nakazawa sisters represent Japan at the Lacrosse World Championship in Tokyo
summary: The daughters of former soccer player Yuji Nakazawa compete in the tournament, which also serves as preparation for the 2028 Los Angeles Olympics.

title: The 'alternate capital' bill begins its process in the Senate with concessions from the ruling party
summary: The processing of the law proceeds amidst criticism over lack of time and opposition from some sectors.
